name: Full local backup + test baseline
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei (a Python MCP server project with venv at .venv/).

Do the following:
1. Create a full backup: cp -a /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei /home/fi/backups/memoreei-pre-refactor-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
2. Create a git bundle: git bundle create /home/fi/backups/memoreei-pre-refactor.bundle --all
3. Run the test suite and save output: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v > logs/pre-refactor-tests.log 2>&1
4. Record the current git SHA: git rev-parse HEAD > logs/pre-refactor-sha.txt
5. Create a git tag: git tag v0.1.0-hackathon -m 'Pre-refactor hackathon state'
6. mkdir -p /home/fi/backups and verify all backups exist

Do NOT push the tag yet.
test: test -d /home/fi/backups/memoreei-pre-refactor-* && test -f /home/fi/backups/memoreei-pre-refactor.bundle && test -f logs/pre-refactor-tests.log && test -f logs/pre-refactor-sha.txt && git tag -l v0.1.0-hackathon | grep -q v0.1.0-hackathon

name: Create .internal/ directory structure
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei.

Create the .internal/ directory for agent context and machine-specific notes (will be gitignored):

1. mkdir -p .internal/archived

2. Copy these files to .internal/archived/ (keep originals for now — we'll git rm them later):
   - cp PLAN.md .internal/archived/
   - cp PROGRESS.md .internal/archived/
   - cp DEMO.md .internal/archived/
   - cp TASK.md .internal/archived/

3. Copy these to .internal/ root as living docs:
   - cp ENTRY.md .internal/
   - cp CLAUDE.md .internal/

4. Create .internal/SETUP.md with our machine-specific setup info:
```
# Machine Setup — Karamja

> This file is gitignored. It holds machine-specific deployment details.
> No secrets or tokens — those stay in .env

## Host
- Hostname: karamja
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Tailscale IP: REDACTED_IP

## Paths
- Project: /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei
- Venv: .venv/bin/python
- DB: ./memoreei.db

## Discord (Karamja Server)
| Channel | ID |
|---------|----|
| #memoreei-demo | REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID |
| #project-updates | REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID |
| #project-live | REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID |
| #projects | REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID_4 |
| Server ID | REDACTED_SERVER_ID |
| Elliot bot user | REDACTED_BOT_ID |
| Caleb user | REDACTED_USER_ID |

## Bot Tokens
All in Bitwarden: 'discord elliot', 'discord fi', 'discord navi', 'discord ford'

## Services
- Movie Ring: port 5050
- Contact Dossier: port 5051
- watch_and_sync.py: auto-syncs DB on Caleb's Discord messages

## Git
- SSH key: /home/fi/.ssh/id_ed25519_github
- Remote: git@github.com:CalebChristiansen/Memoreei.git
- Git user: REDACTED_EMAIL / Fi (Elliot's Build Bot)
```

5. Update .internal/ENTRY.md — add this header at the very top:
```
<!-- 
  THIS DIRECTORY IS GITIGNORED.
  It holds agent context, machine-specific config, and deployment notes.
  No secrets or tokens — those stay in .env
  Keep this file updated as the project evolves.
-->
```

6. Add .internal/ to .gitignore if not already there. Add it on a new line at the end of the file.
test: test -d .internal/archived && test -f .internal/ENTRY.md && test -f .internal/SETUP.md && test -f .internal/archived/PLAN.md && test -f .internal/archived/DEMO.md && grep -q '.internal/' .gitignore

name: Remove hardcoded personal data from source files
prompt:

name: Scrub sensitive strings from git history
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei.

IMPORTANT: This rewrites git history. A full backup already exists at /home/fi/backups/.

1. Install git-filter-repo if not present:
   pip install git-filter-repo
   (or check if it's available: git-filter-repo --version)

2. Create a replacements file at /tmp/memoreei-replacements.txt with these lines (format: literal:FIND==>REPLACE):

literal:REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID==>REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID
literal:REDACTED_SERVER_ID==>REDACTED_SERVER_ID
literal:REDACTED_USER_ID==>REDACTED_USER_ID
literal:REDACTED_BOT_ID==>REDACTED_BOT_ID
literal:REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID==>REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID
literal:REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID==>REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID
literal:REDACTED_IP==>REDACTED_IP
literal:REDACTED_EMAIL==>REDACTED_EMAIL

3. Run: git filter-repo --replace-text /tmp/memoreei-replacements.txt --force

4. Re-add the remote (filter-repo removes it):
   git remote add origin git@github.com:CalebChristiansen/Memoreei.git

5. Verify by searching the full history:
   git log -p --all | grep -c 'REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID' || true
   git log -p --all | grep -c '100\.110\.148\.104' || true
   git log -p --all | grep -c 'REDACTED_USER' || true
   All should return 0.

6. Do NOT push yet — we'll verify everything first.

Note: This will change all commit SHAs. The v0.1.0-hackathon tag may be lost — that's fine, we have the bundle backup.
test: test $(git log -p --all 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID' || echo 0) -eq 0 && test $(git log -p --all 2>/dev/null | grep -c '100\.110\.148\.104' || echo 0) -eq 0 && test $(git log -p --all 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'REDACTED_USER' || echo 0) -eq 0 && git remote -v | grep -q 'origin'

name: Post-scrub verification + test suite
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei.

Git history has been rewritten. Verify everything still works:

1. Run the full test suite:
   .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v
   All 21 tests must pass. If any fail, diagnose and fix.

2. Verify the MCP server starts:
   timeout 5 .venv/bin/python -m memoreei.server 2>&1 || true
   (It runs on stdio so it will hang — timeout is fine. Just verify no import errors.)

3. Search ALL tracked files for any remaining sensitive strings:
   git ls-files | xargs grep -l 'REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID' 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLEAN'
   git ls-files | xargs grep -l '100\.110\.148\.104' 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLEAN'
   git ls-files | xargs grep -l 'REDACTED_USER' 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLEAN'
   git ls-files | xargs grep -l 'REDACTED_USER_ID' 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLEAN'
   git ls-files | xargs grep -l 'REDACTED_SERVER_ID' 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLEAN'
   All should print CLEAN.

4. If any tests fail due to the scrub (e.g., test fixtures referencing old IDs), fix them.

5. Commit any fixes: git commit -am 'fix: post-scrub test fixes'

6. Push everything (force push since history was rewritten):
   git push --force origin main
   git push --tags --force origin 2>/dev/null || true
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed' && test $(git ls-files | xargs grep -c 'REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID' 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -eq 0

name: Create centralized config module
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create a centralized configuration system:

1. Create src/memoreei/config.py:
- Load .env once using python-dotenv
- Define a Config dataclass with typed fields for all settings:
  - db_path: str (default: './memoreei.db')
  - embedding_provider: str (default: 'fastembed')
  - openai_api_key: str | None
  - auto_sync: bool (default: False)
  - sync_interval: int (default: 300, in seconds)
  - Discord config: token, channel_id (all optional)
  - Telegram config: token, chat_id (all optional)
  - Matrix config: homeserver, access_token, room_id (all optional)
  - Slack config: bot_token, channel_id (all optional)
  - Email config: email, app_password (all optional)
  - Mastodon config: instance, hashtag, access_token (all optional)
- A get_config() function that returns a singleton Config instance
- A method configured_connectors() -> list[str] that returns names of connectors with sufficient config

2. Update src/memoreei/server.py to use get_config() instead of direct os.environ calls for db_path and embedding_provider. Keep the existing tool functions working — don't break anything.

3. Make sure all 21 existing tests pass: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

4. Commit: git commit -am 'refactor: centralized config module'
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/python -c 'from memoreei.config import get_config; c = get_config(); print(c.db_path, c.embedding_provider)' && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed'

name: Overhaul sync strategy — on-demand only
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Refactor the sync strategy to be on-demand instead of aggressive background polling:

1. Modify src/memoreei/sync_manager.py:
   - Remove the automatic background_loop that polls Discord every 60 seconds
   - Remove the maybe_refresh() call before searches (was hitting Discord API on every search)
   - Keep the SyncManager class but make it simpler:
     - refresh_all() still works for explicit calls
     - Add sync_source(source_name) to sync a specific connector
     - Track last_sync_time per source (not global)
   - Add opt-in background sync controlled by config:
     - Only start background loop if config.auto_sync is True
     - Use config.sync_interval (default 300s = 5 min) instead of hardcoded 60s

2. Update src/memoreei/server.py:
   - Remove the lifespan background task that starts the polling loop (or make it conditional on auto_sync)
   - The search_memory tool should NOT trigger any sync — just search what's in the DB
   - Remove skip_sync parameter from search_memory (it's always skip now)
   - Keep refresh_memory tool as the explicit way to sync
   - Add a new sync_all tool that syncs every configured connector

3. Make sure all tests pass: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

4. Commit: git commit -am 'refactor: on-demand sync, remove aggressive background polling'
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed' && ! grep -q 'background_loop' src/memoreei/server.py

name: Add CLI with typer
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

1. Add typer to dependencies in pyproject.toml under [project] dependencies.

2. Install it: .venv/bin/pip install typer

3. Create src/memoreei/cli.py with these commands using typer:

   memoreei serve          — Start MCP server (stdio transport, default). Just calls the existing mcp.run()
   memoreei serve --sse --port 8080  — SSE transport (if supported by mcp SDK)
   memoreei status         — Show DB stats (message count, source counts, last sync times), configured connectors
   memoreei sync [source]  — Sync a specific source or all configured sources
   memoreei search 'query' — Search memories from CLI, print results
   memoreei import <file>  — Import a WhatsApp .txt export
   memoreei config         — Show current configuration (which env vars are set, which connectors are ready)

4. Update pyproject.toml entry point:
   [project.scripts]
   memoreei = "memoreei.cli:app"
   (Keep the old entry point too if it exists, but the CLI should be the primary one)

5. Reinstall the package: .venv/bin/pip install -e .

6. Test the CLI works:
   .venv/bin/memoreei --help
   .venv/bin/memoreei config
   .venv/bin/memoreei status

7. Make sure all existing tests still pass: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

8. Commit: git commit -am 'feat: CLI interface with typer (serve, sync, search, status, config, import)'
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/memoreei --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'serve' && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed'

name: Create CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei.

Create contributor documentation:

1. Create CONTRIBUTING.md at project root:
   - How to set up dev environment (clone, venv, pip install -e '.[dev]')
   - How to run tests (pytest)
   - Code style (type hints, docstrings)
   - How to add a new connector (implement BaseConnector in connectors/)
   - PR process (fork, branch, test, PR)
   - Keep it concise and welcoming

2. Create docs/ directory with:
   - docs/architecture.md — System design overview:
     - How hybrid search works (BM25 + vector + RRF fusion)
     - Database schema
     - Connector architecture (BaseConnector interface)
     - MCP tool flow
   - docs/connectors.md — Guide to adding a new connector:
     - Implement BaseConnector
     - Register in __init__.py
     - Add config fields
     - Add MCP tool
     - Add tests
   - docs/deployment.md — Self-hosting guide:
     - Bare metal (pip install, systemd service)
     - Docker (Dockerfile reference)
     - Configuration reference (all env vars)

3. Create a basic Dockerfile:
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml .
COPY src/ src/
COPY data/ data/
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .
ENV MEMOREEI_DB_PATH=/data/memoreei.db
VOLUME /data
ENTRYPOINT ["memoreei"]
CMD ["serve"]
```

4. Create docker-compose.yml:
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  memoreei:
    build: .
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data
      - ./.env:/app/.env:ro
    environment:
      - MEMOREEI_DB_PATH=/data/memoreei.db
```

5. Commit: git commit -am 'docs: CONTRIBUTING.md, architecture docs, Dockerfile'
test: test -f CONTRIBUTING.md && test -f docs/architecture.md && test -f docs/connectors.md && test -f docs/deployment.md && test -f Dockerfile && test -f docker-compose.yml

name: iMessage connector (macOS local DB read)
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create an iMessage connector that reads the local macOS Messages database (read-only):

1. Create src/memoreei/connectors/imessage_connector.py:
   - Read ~/Library/Messages/chat.db (SQLite, read-only mode)
   - Path configurable via IMESSAGE_DB_PATH env var (default: ~/Library/Messages/chat.db)
   - Parse the schema: tables 'message', 'handle', 'chat', 'chat_message_join', 'chat_handle_join'
   - Extract: sender (from handle table), message text (from message.text), timestamp (message.date — note: Apple uses 'Apple epoch' = seconds since 2001-01-01, needs conversion), chat/group name
   - Handle both iMessage and SMS (both in same DB)
   - Checkpoint-based: track last ROWID processed per chat
   - Only works on macOS — detect platform and fail gracefully on Linux/Windows with a clear message
   - Open DB in read-only mode: sqlite3.connect(f'file:{db_path}?mode=ro', uri=True)

2. Add checkpoint table in main database.py — add an imessage_checkpoint table:
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS imessage_checkpoint (
       chat_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
       last_rowid INTEGER NOT NULL,
       updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
   )

3. Add sync_imessage MCP tool in server.py:
   @mcp.tool()
   async def sync_imessage(chat_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
       """Sync iMessage/SMS conversations from the local macOS Messages database.
       Reads ~/Library/Messages/chat.db (read-only). macOS only.
       Args:
           chat_name: Optional filter — only sync messages from this chat/contact.
       """

4. Add to connector registry in connectors/__init__.py

5. Add config fields to config.py: imessage_db_path

6. Write tests in tests/test_imessage.py:
   - Test parsing logic with a mock/fake chat.db created in-memory
   - Test platform detection (should indicate unsupported on Linux)
   - Test checkpoint tracking
   - Do NOT require actual macOS Messages DB for tests

7. Run all tests: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

8. Commit: git commit -am 'feat: iMessage connector — read-only local macOS Messages DB'

NOTE: We are on Linux, so the connector won't have a real chat.db to read. That's fine — write it correctly for macOS and test with mocks.
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/python -c 'from memoreei.connectors.imessage_connector import *; print("OK")' && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed'

name: Discord Data Package import connector
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create a Discord Data Package import connector. This parses the GDPR data export from Discord (the ZIP or extracted folder that users request at Discord Settings > Privacy & Safety > Request All of My Data). This gives access to ALL channels, ALL servers, ALL DMs — not just one channel.

1. Create src/memoreei/connectors/discord_package_connector.py:
   - Accept a path to the extracted data package folder (or ZIP file — extract if needed)
   - Structure: messages/ directory with subdirectories per channel, each containing messages.json
   - Each messages.json has array of: {ID, Timestamp, Contents, Attachments}
   - Also parse: messages/index.json which maps channel IDs to channel names
   - Extract: message content, sender (from Authors field), timestamp, channel name as source
   - Source format: 'discord-export:<channel_name>' or 'discord-export:<channel_id>'
   - Deduplicate against existing discord: sources (same message ID = skip)
   - Handle large exports efficiently (stream/chunk, don't load all into memory)

2. Add MCP tool in server.py:
   @mcp.tool()
   async def import_discord_package(package_path: str) -> dict:
       """Import a Discord Data Package (GDPR export). Imports all messages from all channels and DMs.
       Request your data at Discord Settings > Privacy & Safety > Request All of My Data.
       Args:
           package_path: Path to extracted data package folder or ZIP file
       """

3. Also add CLI command: memoreei import discord-package <path>
   Update the CLI in cli.py to support this.

4. Write tests in tests/test_discord_package.py:
   - Create a minimal fake data package structure in a temp dir
   - Test parsing messages.json format
   - Test channel name resolution from index.json
   - Test deduplication

5. Run all tests: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

6. Commit: git commit -am 'feat: Discord Data Package import — all channels, DMs, servers'
test: cd /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei && .venv/bin/python -c 'from memoreei.connectors.discord_package_connector import *; print("OK")' && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q 'passed'

name: SMS Backup & Restore XML import connector
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create an SMS/text message import connector for Android SMS Backup & Restore app XML format:

1. Create src/memoreei/connectors/sms_connector.py:
   - Parse XML files from 'SMS Backup & Restore' app (most popular Android SMS backup — 10M+ downloads)
   - XML format: <smses> root with <sms> elements containing attributes:
     - address: phone number
     - date: unix timestamp in milliseconds
     - type: 1=received, 2=sent
     - body: message text
     - contact_name: sender name (may be empty)
     - readable_date: human readable date string
   - Also handle <mms> elements (multimedia messages) — extract text parts
   - Source format: 'sms:<contact_name_or_number>'
   - Use xml.etree.ElementTree for parsing (stdlib, no extra deps)

name: Signal Desktop connector (SQLCipher local DB)
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create a Signal Desktop connector that reads the local encrypted database:

1. Create src/memoreei/connectors/signal_connector.py:
   - Signal Desktop stores messages in an SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite database
   - Locations by OS:
     - Linux: ~/.config/Signal/sql/db.sqlite
     - macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Signal/sql/db.sqlite
     - Windows: %APPDATA%\Signal\sql\db.sqlite
   - The encryption key is stored in config.json in the same Signal directory (field: 'key')
   - Auto-detect OS and find the DB + key automatically
   - Config overrides: SIGNAL_DB_PATH, SIGNAL_CONFIG_PATH
   - Use pysqlcipher3 for encrypted DB access (add as optional dependency)
   - If pysqlcipher3 is not installed, try using sqlcipher CLI as fallback
   - If neither available, return clear error: 'Signal connector requires pysqlcipher3: pip install pysqlcipher3'
   - Tables: messages (with conversationId, body, sent_at, source, type), conversations
   - Checkpoint: track last ROWID per conversation
   - Read-only access — never write to Signal's DB

2. Add pysqlcipher3 as optional dependency in pyproject.toml:
   [project.optional-dependencies]
   signal = ["pysqlcipher3>=1.0.0"]

3. Add MCP tool in server.py:
   @mcp.tool()
   async def sync_signal(conversation_id: str | None = None) -> dict:
       """Sync Signal Desktop messages from the local encrypted database.
       Requires Signal Desktop to be installed and pysqlcipher3 package.
       Args:
           conversation_id: Optional filter for a specific conversation.
       """

4. Add config fields to config.py

5. Write tests in tests/test_signal.py:
   - Test OS detection logic for DB path
   - Test key extraction from config.json (mock the file)
   - Test that missing pysqlcipher3 gives clear error
   - Do NOT require actual Signal Desktop installation for tests

6. Run all tests: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

7. Commit: git commit -am 'feat: Signal Desktop connector — local encrypted DB read'

name: Instagram Data Download import connector
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei. Venv at .venv/.

Create an Instagram data download import connector:

1. Create src/memoreei/connectors/instagram_connector.py:
   - Parse Instagram's GDPR data export (JSON format)
   - User downloads from: Settings > Accounts Center > Your Information > Download Your Information > JSON format
   - Structure: your_instagram_activity/messages/inbox/<conversation_name>/message_1.json (can be multiple parts)
   - Each message_N.json contains:
     { "participants": [{"name": "..."}], "messages": [{"sender_name": "...", "timestamp_ms": 123, "content": "...", "type": "Generic"}] }
   - Note: Instagram uses UTF-8 encoding but represents special chars as latin-1 escaped. You may need to decode: text.encode('latin-1').decode('utf-8')
   - Source format: 'instagram:<conversation_name>'
   - Handle multi-part exports (message_1.json, message_2.json, etc.)
   - Extract: sender, content, timestamp, conversation name
   - Skip non-text messages (photos, videos, etc.) — just mark as [media]

2. Add MCP tool in server.py:

   async def import_instagram(data_path: str) -> dict:

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

name: Remove usecases/ from main repo, add examples/
prompt:
You are working in /home/fi/.openclaw/workspace-elliot/projects/memoreei.

Clean up the main repo now that use case apps are extracted:

1. Remove usecases/ directory from git:
   git rm -r usecases/

2. Create examples/ directory with simple, generic examples:

   examples/basic_search.py:
   ```python
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   """Example: search Memoreei memory database directly (without MCP)."""
   import asyncio
   from memoreei.storage.database import Database
   from memoreei.search.embeddings import get_provider
   from memoreei.search.hybrid import HybridSearch
   
   async def main():
       db = Database(db_path='./memoreei.db')
       await db.connect()
       embedder = get_provider()
       search = HybridSearch(db=db, embedder=embedder)
       results = await search.search('what did we talk about?', limit=5)
       for r in results:
           print(f"[{r.get('source', '?')}] {r.get('content', '')[:100]}")
   
   if __name__ == '__main__':
       asyncio.run(main())
   ```

   examples/discord_bot.py:
   A simplified, generic version of the memory bot. No hardcoded IDs. Reads DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN from env. Responds to !remember <query> with search results.

3. Update README.md to mention the example app repos:
   Add a section 'Example Apps' with links (placeholder URLs for now):
   - [Movie Ring](https://github.com/CalebChristiansen/memoreei-movie-ring) — Discover what movies your friends are talking about
   - [Contact Dossier](https://github.com/CalebChristiansen/memoreei-contact-dossier) — CRM for your real relationships

4. Commit: git commit -am 'chore: remove usecases/, add examples/, link to extracted app repos'
